operating_state: Total revenue decreased $10 million year-over-year driven by mixed passenger demand patterns, with premium products and loyalty travel awards increasing while main cabin declined due to reduced consumer and corporate confidence from macroeconomic uncertainty early in the quarter. Operating income fell $165 million to $2.1 billion despite capacity expansion of 4%. Adjusted total revenue increased $100 million or 1% excluding refinery sales to third parties.
cost_structure: Total operating expense increased $155 million or 1% driven by 4% capacity expansion, higher employee wages, and increased landing fees and rents, partially offset by lower aircraft fuel costs. Total operating cost per available seat mile decreased 3% while non-fuel unit costs increased 2.7%. Adjusted operating expense increased $320 million or 2% primarily from capacity growth offset by fuel savings.
material_events: Board authorized $1 billion share repurchase program through June 2028 as part of $2 billion three-year shareholder return plan. Completed $2.0 billion unsecured notes offering with proceeds used to repay Payroll Support Program loan due 2030. Announced planned minority stake acquisition in WestJet alongside Korean Air partnership. Shareholders approved amended Performance Compensation Plan extending through 2035 with 9.6 million additional shares authorized.
macro_exposures: Macroeconomic uncertainty impacted consumer and corporate travel confidence particularly early in the June quarter, resulting in main cabin revenue decline despite overall passenger revenue growth. No material changes to previously disclosed risk factors were reported during the period.
forward_outlook: Management maintains capital allocation framework targeting 1x gross leverage and unencumbered assets exceeding $40 billion with priorities of business reinvestment, debt reduction, and increasing shareholder returns as leverage targets approach. Share repurchase authorization provides flexibility during market volatility with commitment to steady dividend growth.
uncertainty_profile: Standard forward-looking statement risks remain unchanged including aircraft fuel cost volatility, technology system security, supply chain disruptions, labor relations, environmental regulations, and geopolitical conflicts. Refinery operations face regulatory compliance costs and insurance coverage uncertainties.
